_meta.source: BIR Revenue Regulations No. 29-2025 (issued 2025-12-22), further amending the de minimis benefit ceilings under RR 2-98 / RR 11-2018
notes: De minimis benefits are non-taxable up to the listed cap. Amounts in excess of the cap are subject to income tax (and, for managerial/supervisory employees, may also be subject to fringe benefit tax).
These caps are SEPARATE from and STACK ON TOP OF the ₱90,000 annual exemption for 13th-month pay + other bonuses.
Per-category treatment is mandatory — they cannot be aggregated. An employee receiving rice subsidy ₱3,000/mo cannot offset the ₱500 excess with unused laundry allowance.
OT meal allowance (30% of regional minimum wage) is not represented as a fixed peso cap because it varies by region; callers must supply both amount and regional wage when checking.

rice_subsidy.description: Rice subsidy of ₱2,500 or one 50-kg sack of rice per month, whichever is non-cash

medical_cash_allowance_dependents.description: Medical cash allowance to dependents — ₱2,000 per semester, expressed here as monthly equivalent (₱2,000 / 6 months ≈ ₱333.33)

employee_achievement_awards.description: Employee achievement awards (tangible personal property, not cash, for length-of-service or safety achievement)

gifts_christmas_anniversary.description: Gifts given during Christmas and major anniversary celebrations

cba_productivity_incentives.description: CBA benefits and productivity incentives combined (single ceiling for both)

monetized_unused_vl_private.description: Monetized unused vacation leave credits for private employees — up to 12 days per year, valued at the employee's daily rate

annual_exemption_13th_month_and_bonuses.source: RA 10963 (TRAIN Law), Section 32(B)(7)(e); confirmed unchanged as of RR 29-2025
annual_exemption_13th_month_and_bonuses.description: 13th-month pay, Christmas bonus, productivity incentives that are NOT classified as de minimis, and other benefits — non-taxable in aggregate up to ₱90,000 per year. Excess is taxable in the year of receipt.
